behavior_summary: Responds to a player 'U8P_BONES2::look' on the skeleton by emitting a short descriptive label ('skeleton ') from the skeleton item/process.
Handles a player 'use' on the skeleton: checks an item status bit (mask 0x0100). If the bit is clear it simply closes any open UI gump for the item and returns.

If the status bit 0x0100 is set, the 'use' handler performs a probabilistic branch using urandom(100): in a low-probability case it opens a gump with parameter 0x0C; otherwise the skeleton shouts 'Do not disturb my rest! ' and the process is suspended (interaction halted).
notable_control_flow: BONES2::look (U8P_BONES2::look event) has a single straight-line behavior: call Item::bark("skeleton ") and then return/suspend as per normal event termination (high confidence).
BONES2::use (use event) branches on a status bit: if (Item::getStatus() & 0x0100) == 0 then call Item::closeGump() and return (high confidence).

When status bit 0x0100 is set, a random choice is made using urandom(0x64). If the returned value > 0x5F (i.e., only on a rare roll) call Item::openGump(0x0C); otherwise call Item::bark("Do not disturb my rest! ") and suspend the process (high confidence on branching logic, medium confidence on exact numeric threshold semantics due to urandom interpretation).
open_questions: What exact gameplay meaning does status bit 0x0100 encode for the skeleton (e.g., 'is hostile', 'has gump open', or 'is awakened')? (inferred, medium confidence).
What is the semantics and UI content of gump id/parameter 0x0C (what UI/window is presented to the player)? (low confidence).
Is the implies/pop sequence after Item::bark used for any gameplay effect beyond normal event termination (likely housekeeping; low confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_BONES2::look
main_function.purpose: Serve the item 'U8P_BONES2::look' (examine) event by making the skeleton item emit a short descriptive label to the viewer.
entry_conditions: Invoked when a player or actor inspects/looks at the skeleton item (Event 0 / U8P_BONES2::look).

function_map.name: U8P_BONES2::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_BONES2::look' event for the skeleton item; call Item::bark with the label 'skeleton '.

function_map.name: U8P_BONES2::use
function_map.role: Handle the 'use' event on the skeleton: check a status bit, possibly close a gump, otherwise run a random branch to either open a gump or bark a warning and suspend.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine API to emit/display a short text message from an item/process to a player/process.

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Engine API returning status bits/flags for the item/process; used to test mask 0x0100.

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Engine API to close the item's UI gump if open.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Engine/random API returning a pseudorandom integer in [0, n-1]; used for probabilistic branching (called with 0x64).

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Engine API to open a UI gump for the item with a supplied parameter (here 0x0C).

external_calls.purpose: Emit the descriptive label 'skeleton ' from the skeleton item to the viewer/process.
Read the item's status bits to test mask 0x0100 and decide control flow.
Close the item's UI gump when the tested status bit is clear (clean-up behavior on 'use').
Generate a pseudorandom value in [0,99] (called with 0x64) to implement rare behavior branching.
Open a specific UI gump (parameter 0x0C) in the rare random branch.
Display a warning message 'Do not disturb my rest! ' when the random check falls into the common branch, prior to suspending the process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Responds to a player 'U8P_BONES2::look' on the skeleton by emitting a short descriptive label ('skeleton ') from the skeleton item/process.
